title: Transgender Treatments: Is Hormone Therapy A Contraindication
filename: Transgender Treatments：  Is Hormone Therapy A Contraindication.en.vtt
excerpt: Sometimes it's part of the decision making process like i just want to see if this will make me happy and then i might go and do something else let's say someone was on a particular uh hormonal treatment are there any kind of do you have any contraindications or anything like tha…

segments.text: sometimes it's part of the decision making process like i just want to see if this will make me happy and then i might go and do something else let's say someone was on a particular uh hormonal treatment are there any kind of do you have any contraindications or anything like that i think practical and i think the usual principles would apply which is you don't want to be doing elective procedures at a point of uncertainty so if you've just started a new medication or just about to have surgery you would suggest leaving it until a point where things are more stable before you start to make unnecessary changes sometimes it's part of the decision making process like i just want to see if this will make me happy and then i might go and do something else and i mean this is where it gets really complex but that is a possible reason why you might do it in a slightly uncertain time as almost as a diagnostic and a therapeutic tool for some people but all needs to be spoken out at length to make sure that you understand exactly the purpose at that particular time but
there are there is nothing black and white contraindication that i'm aware of other than it's more about the psychological contraindications that we need to be careful of

transcript: sometimes it's part of the decision making process like i just want to see if this will make me happy and then i might go and do something else let's say someone was on a particular uh hormonal treatment are there any kind of do you have any contraindications or anything like that i think practical and i think the usual principles would apply which is you don't want to be doing elective procedures at a point of uncertainty so if you've just started a new medication or just about to have surgery you would suggest leaving it until a point where things are more stable before you start to make unnecessary changes sometimes it's part of the decision making process like i just want to see if this will make me happy and then i might go and do something else and i mean this is where it gets really complex but that is a possible reason why you might do it in a slightly uncertain time as almost as a diagnostic and a therapeutic tool for some people but all needs to be spoken out at length to make sure that you understand exactly the purpose at that particular time but there are there is nothing black and white contraindication that i'm aware of other than it's more about the psychological contraindications that we need to be careful of
